characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Ace Pilot: In the TV series. Gwizdo can call himself a fighter pilot in good conscience as the low technological level of this universe and the everyday pilot practice allow this cowardly and weak little guy to have such a reputation for. If he has ever fought dragons (and successfully), mostly he has done it while sitting behind the helm of St. George. Because he has almost no other way to defeat ferocious dragons on his own. Adaptational Ugliness: In the movie, due to the really bad working conditions at the beginning of his career. Just look at this poor little guy: he looks like an early aged and exhausted kid with a cold voice who has been fairly battered by life.Aggravated in the comic books, where three hunters are shown to be still homeless and barely able to take care of themselves. The fact of this makes Gwizdo very angry, which also doesn't help his looks. Aggravated in the comic books, where three hunters are shown to be still homeless and barely able to take care of themselves. The fact of this makes Gwizdo very angry, which also doesn't help his looks. Adaptation Dye-JobwithAbled in the Adaptation: In the movie he suffers from the progressive myopia because of the need to read often in the dark but in the TV series, created in parallel with the film, his vision is perfect and even his eye color hasn't changed for the worse. The circumstances of his healing aren't told, but given the similar changes in Hector's appearance,it can be possible that these two exchanged their eye irises for medical purposes (and possibly that case made their relationship more strained). Affection-Hating Kid: He didn't fall in love until he was around 33 and accidentally met a really beautiful girl. Because since childhood, he has accumulated a huge reserve of distrust of people and he is ready to trust his true thoughts only to the most reliable of his friends. The first experience of falling in love happens suddenly for him, due to the fact that most of the tavern's visitor

characters.name: Lian-Chu
characters.description: Abled in the Adaptation: As in Gwizdo's case, Lian-Chu's health improved in the series compared to the full-length movie. In his early thirties he was able to get rid of his neglected back problems on his own. As a result, he became an experienced masseur and master of acropuncture. All Amazons Want Hercules: Impresses the women of Amazoomia by throwing large heavy objects farther than they can. Bear Hug: One of the most memorable and heart-warming Lian-Chu's abilities. Big Brother Instinct: In relation to all the children he meets, as well as his weaker comrades, especially Gwizdo. Big Guy, Little Guy: Big Guy to Gwizdo's Little Guy.

characters.name: Hector
characters.description: Adaptation Dye-Job: In the movie, he has blue eyes, but in the series they are brown. Also, with age, his claws and hooves change color from black to white, which suggests that in the movie he is still a fairly young dragon. All Animals Are Dogs: Even though Hector is a dragon, he acts like a dog, but gets angry if someone mistakes him for one. Furthermore, Gwizdo tries to pass him off as one often. Beleaguered Assistant: While in the movie he shows himself as a carefree puppy who takes everything from a protected free life, in the series he ends up doing the majority of the grunt work for Gwizdo and Lian-Chu: carrying heavy loads of equipment and supplies, pedalling theSt. George, sharpening weapons, and even (at one point) doing Gwizdo's laundry. While Lian-Chu seems appreciative of Hector's efforts and treats him with respect as a part of the team, Gwizdo often adds insult to injury by loading on the verbal abuse. Hector responds to Gwizdo by grumbling about the situation. Butt-Monkey: Gwizdo treats him like trash. He andMenchicould share notes. Curtains Match the Window: He has blue eyes and blue fur in the movie. In the series,his eyes are brown.

characters.name: Jeanneline
characters.description: Belligerent Sexual Tension: She and Gwizdo bicker constantly and also seem really into each other. Big Beautiful Woman: A very plump and tall middle-aged woman who still looks attractive to many men. Her experience of three weddings suggests that she was a sought-after bride despite great failures in her personal life. Fat and Proud: After going on a hunt with the guys, she tells them that she's never doing that again because it's "bad for her waistline": she lost 20 pounds (not that she looks any different). Fiery Redhead: She has ginger hair and a temper to match. Husky Russkie: She's very large woman with a noticeable Russian accent.

characters.name: Zaza
characters.description: Adorably Precocious Child: She's a very cute child, but also very intelligent and resourceful. She's also a tough negotiator and can out-debate most grown men. Borrowed Catchphrase: She borrows Gwizdo's "Boy, oh boy" catchphrase in "There's No Place Like Home". Cheerful Child: She's always happy and optimistic. Childish Tooth Gap: She has a gap in her teeth, similar to Gwizdo's. Genki Girl: Not as much as Zoria when she was a child but is still very energetic.

characters.name: Leopold
characters.description: Beast of Battle: Zaza likes to ride him and dreams of making a combat assistant out of him... usually in her fantasies, as Leopold is usually not enthusiastic about her ideas.Nevertheless, he willingly acts as a riding war beast for Hector, participating in the rescue of Zaza in the climactic part of "Porkfester's Pigfarm Island". Big Eater: That's why he's grown so big. Bruiser with a Soft Center: He has great destructive potential due to his size, but in contrast, he is a timid and gentle beast. Literally Falling in Love: in "Porkfester's Pigfarm Island", the main plot is triggered by his sudden crush on a beautiful female pig from a nearby farm. Lovable Cowardon the edge ofDirty Coward: Despite his menacing physique, he is a rather timid and cowardly animal in a moment of danger, even when Zaza hopes for his protection. Over time, he builds up his courage in "Porkfester's Pigfarm Island".

characters.name: Zoria (Zoe)
characters.description: Action Girl: She's a dragon huntress with skills almost on a par with Lian-Chu himself. Adaptation Dye-Job: She was blonde in the movie. Badass in Distress: She's captured by a group of people in "Desperately Seeking Zoria".She also gets severely injured during a mission in "The Master of the Dragon" and her riding dragon finds her only to take her home for urgent treatment. She also gets severely injured during a mission in "The Master of the Dragon" and her riding dragon finds her only to take her home for urgent treatment. Blue Blood: As an adult, she resorted to makeover in order not to be recognized by anyone as an escaped little princess.

characters.name: Noble Kayo
characters.description: Bookworm: He was a real book bug in his past, judging by the size of his library. Even if he doesn't use books now, he's a walking library in his own right. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: He is an eccentric old man, whom not every patient soul can handle because of his talkativeness, but he is extremely informed about the valuable properties of dragons and medicines of dragon origin. Disability Immunity: In "Don't Look Now", he is the only one in the Inn who has not been affected by dragon hypnosis, due to his extremely weak eyesight. Glasses of Aging: His barely useful glasses are so huge that they make him look like a giant insect. Hermit Guru: He is a respected and skilled doctor who lives in a lonely house on the outskirts of the forest.

characters.name: Prince Granion de Bismuth (Prince Charming)
characters.description: All-Loving Hero: He is basically very positive towards strangers and crooks, as in his eyes they are all equal in the face of the threat of dragons.Even after Gwizdo ruined Granion's engagement to the princess in "The High Life", this didn't become an obstacle to consolidating friendship of such dissimilar dragon fighters in "Baby in the Family". Blue Blood: He is the last offspring of the ruling dynasty of the Bismuth Kingdom, so his parents are his sponsors and he is always free from the need to earn money. Dumb Blonde: His heroic enthusiasm and habit of posturing on the battlefield irritates Gwizdo no less than the exaltation of blonde Zoe in the film.Before that, according to the first comic book, Gwizdo saw how a similar approach to work destroyed older prince Granion who was also blond. Seeing the risk of repeating this story is a truly "dubious pleasure"... Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: He is a natural blond and a natural altruist by conviction. Heel Realization: In "Baby in the Family", returning to his family palace alive and unharmed (thanks to the Dragon-Hunting Trio), he realizes that he served as a distracting figure for the unfair tax policy of his mother, who saw him only as a tool. EvenGwizdo was sincerly shocked by the level of her greed!

characters.name: Mother Hubbard
characters.description: Dub Name Change: She's known as Poulard in the original French-language version of the show. Glasses of Aging: She wears glasses both because of her frequent work with papers and because of her old age. Reasonable Authority Figure: In the eyes of Lian-Chu and Gwizdo, she did everything possible to prepare the young orphans for going out into the big world, teaching them to work stoically according to their capabilities. Stern Teacher: She was often forced to play this role for bullies who got caught up in bad deeds.

characters.name: George and Gilbert Forestall
characters.description: Big Brother Instinct: Gilbert is not only older, but also bigger than George and acts as an appropriate figure for him despite their frequent squabbles. Big Guy, Little Guy: George's Little Guy to Gilbert's Big Guy. The Bully: Both were inclined to mock Gwizdo from a young age. Even a few decades later, they didn't change this habit, now on the basis of very well hidden envy of his later career successes. Conveniently an Orphan: They also spent their childhood in an orphanage. Those Two Guys: They regularly antagonize Lian-Chu and Gwizdo both on the grounds of professional competition as well as because of the role as bullies many years ago.

characters.name: Billy Toughnut
characters.description: Conveniently an Orphan: Staying at the orphanage for Lian-Chu and Gwizdo, with their regular run-ins with bullies, was relatively bearable in his company. Heroic Build: He has a moderately athletic figure, although he is not much bigger than Gwizdo. Literally Falling in Love: He is in love with the village headman's daughter, even though he lives on the outskirts because of discrimination based on his origin. The Un-Favorite: He was popular in the orphanage, but suffered from discrimination as an adult in the village where he found his vocation because he is not a native. Younger Than They Look: He is not much older than Lian-Chu and Gwizdo, but unlike them, he is already gray-haired.

characters.name: Lord Arnold
characters.description: Blind Weaponmaster: Despite being blind and unable to walk as a result of meeting the World Gobbler decades ago, he is still a very capable master of bladed weapons and doesn't part with his swords and an axe. Blood Knight: He is a dedicated warrior who enjoyed his craft even after gaining a high status. His passion was passed on to his young niece against his own wishes. Broken Bird: After the first major failure with the dragon, he drank the cup of helplessness and sorrow, which led to his fierce attitude towards the apocalypse in case of failure to find suitable warriors for the mission. Cool Uncle: To Zoe, paradoxically, right before his healing because of the Dragon-Hunting Trio's victory Determinator: Having failed in the battle against the apocalyptic threat to all living things, he didn't give up and decided to use all remaining chances to the last.

characters.name: Gildas
characters.description: Dr. Jerk: If you are sure that your emotional well-being should be improved by talking to a doctor during treatment, Gildas is surely not your address. Frontier Doctor: Quite literally. He is living and working in semi-isolation among the Castle on the local far West bordering with dangerous territories full of gravity anomalies and dragons. Hyper-Competent Sidekick: One of the few people who remained loyal to the old lord over the past decades, he not only kept Arnold alive with his medicine, but also kept his estate perfectly clean, including the Trophy Room. No Sense of Humor: He's used to saying only what he thinks, not trying to smooth things out with his imagination. Zoe is annoyed by this, but it's a valuable quality for a cripple's assistant if the patient is only interested in receiving truthful news.

characters.name: Sir Anatole Lensflair
characters.description: One of Lord Arnold's oldest friends and most loyal knights. He was sent to slay the World Gobbler despite his advanced age, being the last remaining warrior of Lord Arnold before he hires Gwizdo and Lian-Chu. However, things don't exactly go well for him, as the heroes discover. He only appears inThe Movie. Knight in Shining Armor: Such an exaggerated reflection of the trope that it is emphasized by his name, which embodies the bright shine of perfectly polished armor.
